body.title: Memory tags via metadata — tag:<name> as new relationship layer
body.result: Shipped in e09e1b0b. Tag layer as metadata convention: tag:<name> keys in memories.metadata JSONB. New module wild_igor/igor/memory/tag_tree.py with pure extract_tag_names + build_tag_tree (slash-separated paths produce nested tree with aggregating _count). Cortex methods: apply_tag, get_tags_for, memories_with_tag, tag_tree. Reuses existing idx_memories_metadata_gin. 17 tests (10 pure + 7 DB-backed).
body.description:
Akien's framing (2026-04-22): 'to put tags on memory, just put them in the metadata. an extra level of linked relationships. and the collection of tags itself can become a tree just from having tag:<name> in the metadata.'

Shape: memories already have a JSONB metadata column. Add convention: any key with prefix 'tag:' (e.g. tag:workflow, tag:debugging, tag:anticipation) marks a tag on that memory. Collection of all tag:* keys across memories implicitly forms a tag-tree (hierarchy emerges when tags share prefixes like tag:pe_chain/situate, tag:pe_chain/hypothesize). No new table, no new memory type.

Minimum needed:
1. A tag-read helper: get_tags_for(memory_id) -> list[str] and memories_with_tag(tag) -> list[memory_id]. Index the tag-value pattern in JSONB for fast lookup (gin index on metadata with path ops).
2. A tag-write helper on cortex.store or a tag-apply tool.
3. A tag-tree summariser: tag_tree() -> dict returns nested dict of all tags, counts per leaf.

This is about adding a lightweight relationship layer that Igor can use without promoting tags to a full schema. Tags are descriptive metadata, not edges in the graph.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/memory/cortex.py (tag helpers), wild_igor/igor/memory/tag_tree.py (new module for tree assembly), tests/test_memory_tags.py (new).
**Design rules:** no-new-memory-schemas (tags live in existing metadata JSONB — compliant by design), test-plan-or-why-not.
**Scope boundary:** IN — tag read/write helpers, tag-tree summariser, gin index on metadata. OUT — UI/dashboard tag browser, tag-driven auto-retrieval (separate concern — let wandering-search use tags as candidates).
**Test plan:** (a) setting metadata['tag:foo']=true on a memory and fetching memories_with_tag('foo') returns it; (b) hierarchical tags (tag:a/b, tag:a/c) produce correct nested tree; (c) tag counts are accurate across a small fixture set.
